name: Purushottama
dates_notes: Sectarian dates yield an implausible 113-year span; most scholars adjust the bounds.
core_thesis:
Puruṣottama is the principal scholastic of the post-Viṭṭhalanātha *Puṣṭi-Mārga*. Vallabha's *śuddhādvaita* is preserved without doctrinal alteration: Brahman-as-Kṛṣṇa as the sole reality (*Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* on *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 1.1.2](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.2)); *jīva* and *jagat* as real *aṃśas* through *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* (*Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* on *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.3.43](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.3.43); *Prasthāna-Ratnākara*, *jīva-prakaraṇa*); the rejection of *māyā-vāda* and *jagat-mithyātva* (*Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga* throughout, especially the *avaraṇa-niṣedha-prakaraṇa* and the *anirvacanīyatā-khaṇḍana*); *Puṣṭi* as the operative soteriological principle (*Prasthāna-Ratnākara*, *anugraha-prakaraṇa*; cf. Vallabha, *Puṣṭi-Pravāha-Maryādā-Bheda*).

The contribution is technical and is concentrated in three works. The *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga* is a sustained refutation of Advaitic *māyā-vāda* on its own technical ground, dismantling the *avaraṇa-śakti* of *māyā* — the alleged power by which Brahman is concealed — and the *vivartavāda* it underwrites (*Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*, *avaraṇa-śakti-niṣedha* and *vivarta-niṣedha* sections). The *Prasthāna-Ratnākara* is an encyclopaedic Puṣṭimārga doctrinal compendium gathering the school's positions across the *prasthāna-traya* (*Upaniṣad-prasthāna*, *Sūtra-prasthāna*, *Smṛti-prasthāna* sections). The *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* is the sub-commentary on Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* that opens out the bhāṣya's compressed glosses at full scholastic pitch (especially on [BS 1.1.2; 2.1.14; 2.3.43; 4.4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.2; 2.1.14; 2.3.43; 4.4)).

The organising concern is demarcation. *Śuddhādvaita* — non-dualism *with* a real world — is sometimes carelessly conflated with Śaṅkara's Advaita on the strength of the shared *advaita* label. Puruṣottama's task across the three works is to demonstrate that *śuddhādvaita* is not a confused intermediate between Advaita and dualism but a settled and internally coherent Vedāntic option, distinct from Advaita on the world's reality (*Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*; *Prasthāna-Ratnākara*, *jagat-prakaraṇa*), distinct from Viśiṣṭādvaita on the necessity of the *śarīra-śarīrin* qualification (*Prasthāna-Ratnākara*, *Brahma-svarūpa-prakaraṇa*), distinct from Madhva-Dvaita on the *aṃśa*-relation, distinct from the *bhedābheda* lines on whether *bheda* requires *upādhi* or is intrinsic. The school's signature thesis is *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* (Vallabha, *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha*, *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa*; *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* on *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.1.14](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.1.14)); the demarcations follow from it.

engaged_works.title: Prasthana-Ratnakara
engaged_works.summary: The *Prasthāna-Ratnākara* ("ocean of the *prasthāna*") is Puruṣottama's encyclopaedic *Puṣṭi-Mārga* doctrinal compendium. The organisation is a systematic survey of the *prasthāna-traya* — Upaniṣads, Bhagavad-Gītā, Brahma-Sūtras — read consistently through *śuddhādvaita*. Three tasks structure the work. It assembles Vallabha's and Viṭṭhalanātha's settled positions in synoptic form. It locates those positions in the wider Vedānta landscape, with explicit demarcation against rival schools. It stages the school's response to the standard objections from Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, the *bhedābheda* lines, and the Caitanya school. Topics treated include the *pramāṇa*-status of the *Bhāgavata* (*Prasthāna-Ratnākara*, *pramāṇa-prakaraṇa*), the structure of *avikṛta-pariṇāma* (*jagat-prakaraṇa*), the *jīva*'s *aṃśa*-status with concealment of *ānanda* (*jīva-prakaraṇa*), the typology of *jīvas* — *puṣṭi*, *maryādā*, *pravāha* (*jīva-bheda-prakaraṇa*) — and the operation of *anugraha* (*anugraha-prakaraṇa*), and the conditions and stages of liberation (*mokṣa-prakaraṇa*). The text is the school's mature scholastic instrument and the principal Puṣṭimārga reference for any sustained inter-school doctrinal engagement after the early-modern period.

engaged_works.title: Avarana-Bhanga
engaged_works.summary: The *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga* ("breaking of the veil") is Puruṣottama's sustained polemical treatise against Advaitic *māyā-vāda*. The title is doctrinally exact: he undertakes to dismantle the Advaitic *avaraṇa-śakti* of *māyā* — the alleged power by which Brahman is concealed — and the *vivartavāda* it underwrites. The argument is structured as two complementary critiques. The first is internal: *māyā* as conceived by post-Śaṅkara Advaita is incoherent on its own terms. Puruṣottama works through the *anirvacanīyatā* doctrine (*Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*, *anirvacanīyatā-niṣedha-prakaraṇa*), the *dṛṣṭi-sṛṣṭi* mechanism (*dṛṣṭi-sṛṣṭi-niṣedha*), and the relation between *avidyā* and Brahman (*āśraya-viṣaya-vicāra*), arguing in each case that the Advaitin's commitments cannot be jointly maintained. The second is structural: *māyā* is in any case unnecessary once Vallabha's *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* is admitted (*Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*, concluding *siddhānta-prakaraṇa*; cf. *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* on *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ad [BS 2.1.14](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/2.1.14)), which preserves Brahman's non-dual status without making the world illusory. The work is important precisely because *śuddhādvaita* is sometimes confused with Advaita; the *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga* enforces the demarcation in technical detail. It is the most technically ambitious Puṣṭimārga polemical work and a key text in the school's late-medieval doctrinal self-positioning.

engaged_works.title: Bhashya-Prakasha on Anu-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Vallabha's Aṇu-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: The *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* is Puruṣottama's full sub-commentary on Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras*. Vallabha's bhāṣya is famously terse — true to its name. Puruṣottama opens it out *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa*, supplying the *pūrvapakṣa-uttarapakṣa* construction at full technical pitch (notably on [BS 1.1.1–4](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1.1-4) *catuḥsūtrī*; 2.1.14 *tad-ananyatva-adhikaraṇa*; 2.3.43 *aṃśa-adhikaraṇa*), the precise demarcations against rival Vedānta interpretations, and the connections to the wider *śuddhādvaita* doctrinal corpus. A second function of the work is to clarify the doctrinal continuities between Vallabha's portion of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* ([BS 1.1–3.2.33](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/1.1-3.2.33)) and Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation ([BS 3.2.34–4.4.22](cite://mandana/brahma-siddhi/3.2.34-4.4.22)), treating the bhāṣya as a unified Puṣṭimārga reading of the *sūtras* rather than as a composite of two voices. The *Bhāṣya-Prakāśa* is the canonical late-Puṣṭimārga sub-commentary and the standard scholastic instrument for serious study of the school's Brahma-Sūtra commentary.
